brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is the Bethe/gauge correspondence — Bethe-ansatz-integrable spin chains identified with supersymmetric N=2 gauge-theory vacua (Yangian/quantum-affine/elliptic symmetries), i.e. exact integrability. Paper B's machinery is the spectral phase transition (phonon vs. saddle) of random Euclidean matrices applied to the glass/mode-coupling dynamical transition. The only overlap is the word 'supersymmetric', but it denotes two unrelated things — a physical N=2 gauge symmetry in A versus a supersymmetric disorder-averaging trick in B — so no role-for-role mapping between integrable-spin-chain and random-matrix-spectrum machinery holds.
